text: MarketReel — Global Film Distribution Intelligence Saudi ArabiaFilm Censorship & Certification GuidelinesGeneral Commission for Audiovisual Media (GCAM) — Regulatory Intelligence1. Regulatory Authority & ContextThe General Commission for Audiovisual Media (GCAM) regulates all film and audiovisual content in Saudi Arabia. Saudi Arabia reintroduced commercial cinema in 2018 after a 35-year ban under the Vision 2030 reform programme. While the market is rapidly expanding, the regulatory framework remains rooted in Islamic law (Sharia) principles and enforcement can be inconsistent across chains and regions. All theatrical releases require GCAM approval.2. Rating SystemRatingAudienceDescriptionAgent RuleGGeneralSuitable for all agesApprove for all audiences. No restrictions.PGParental GuidanceGuidance suggested for under-13Approve; mild content advisory.PG-1313Parental guidance strongly recommended under 13Approve with review; moderate content.1515Not suitable under 15Flag mature themes. Age-verification marketing required.18Adults OnlyAdults only — very restrictedRestricted theatrical. Very limited venues. Heavy regulatory scrutiny.BANNEDNoneNot approved for any exhibition in Saudi ArabiaDo not release. Evaluate other territories only.3. Absolutely Prohibited ContentAny content that insults, mocks, or disrespects Islam, the Quran, the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), or Islamic religious practice in any form. This is both a censorship ground and a criminal offence under Saudi law.Any content that insults or disrespects the Saudi royal family or the ruling Al-Saud dynasty.Any content that promotes or depicts atheism, agnosticism, or apostasy from Islam.Any content depicting, promoting, or normalising homosexuality, same-sex relationships, or gender non-conforming identity. LGBTQ content of any kind is prohibited.Explicit sexual content — nudity, sexual acts, or heavily suggestive sexual behaviour.Content depicting or promoting Zionism or portraying Israel positively. Note: Abraham Accords 2020 have partially shifted UAE stance; Saudi Arabia has not formally normalised — monitor diplomatic developments.Content promoting or glorifying terrorism, particularly any framing sympathetic to Islamist extremism.Content that promotes drug or alcohol use.Content advocating for or depicting political opposition to the Saudi government or Arab Gulf monarchies.4. Conditional Content — Edit RequiredContent CategoryGCAM StanceRequired ModificationFemale characters in revealing clothingHIGH sensitivityPrepare Saudi-specific poster and trailer assets with covered costumes. Theatrical cut: assess scene-by-scene.Heterosexual kissing and romance (non-explicit)MEDIUMBrief kissing generally permitted post-2018. Prolonged or passionate scenes: edit likely required. Bedroom scenes: cut.Alcohol consumption on screenMEDIUM–HIGHScenes showing alcohol positively: remove or add negative consequence framing. Bar background: case-by-case.Witchcraft or sorceryMEDIUMFantasy entertainment framing (superhero magic) generally acceptable. Depicted as real occult practice: HIGH risk.Cross-dressing or gender ambiguityHIGHRemove or significantly reduce. Extreme sensitivity.Jewish or Israeli charactersMEDIUM — evolvingMonitor current diplomatic context. Post-Abraham Accords context differs in UAE vs. Saudi Arabia.Violence — standard actionLOWGenerally acceptable. Extreme gore or torture: trim required.Music with explicit or offensive lyricsMEDIUMReplace or mute music containing profane, sexually explicit, or religiously offensive lyrics.5. Marketing & Advertising RulesTheatrical posters must not show female characters in revealing dress. Saudi-specific poster variants are standard practice for all major international studios.Trailers shown in Saudi cinemas must follow the same content rules as the theatrical cut.Out-of-home advertising (billboards, mall displays) requires pre-approval from the Ministry of Media.Social media advertising targeting Saudi users must comply with GCAM digital advertising standards.6. Ramadan Window ConsiderationsFilm content scheduled during Ramadan is expected to align with Islamic values — family-appropriate, morally uplifting content strongly preferred.Action, horror, or hedonistic content performs poorly during Ramadan and may face heightened scrutiny.G and PG-rated family and animated films perform strongly during Ramadan.AGENT RAMADAN RULEAvoid scheduling releases of films with adult content, alcohol, or LGBTQ elements during Ramadan. Time contentious titles to post-Eid Al-Fitr window for best regulatory and commercial outcomes.7. OTT & StreamingNetflix, Amazon Prime Video, Disney operate in Saudi Arabia and must comply with GCAM content standards.Local platform: Shahid (MBC Group) — primary Arabic-language streaming service with high cultural sensitivity standards.GCAM has authority to require removal of non-compliant content from streaming platforms operating in Saudi Arabia.8. Agent Decision Framework — Saudi ArabiaTrigger Detected in FilmRisk LevelRecommended ActionLGBTQ content of any kindHIGH — BANNEDRemove entirely. No Saudi release possible with any LGBTQ content.Islamic religious mockeryHIGH — BANNED CRIMINALDo not submit. Complete removal required or abandon Saudi release entirely.Explicit sexual contentHIGH — BANNEDRemove all explicit scenes for Saudi theatrical consideration.Alcohol or drugs positively portrayedHIGHRemove or add strong negative consequence and disclaimer.Female characters in revealing dressMEDIUMPrepare Saudi-specific marketing assets. Film cut: assess scene by scene.Heterosexual romance (non-explicit)LOW–MEDIUMAcceptable with post-2018 GCAM reforms. Flag prolonged kissing for review.Violence or action sequencesLOWGenerally acceptable. Extreme gore: trim required.Criticism of Saudi or Gulf governmentsHIGH — BANNEDAny criticism of royal family or government: abandon Saudi release.Witchcraft shown as realMEDIUMReframe as entertainment fantasy or remove.MarketReel Confidential Saudi Arabia Censorship Guidelines For Agent Reasoning & Internal Distribution Use Only
